text.title: Freelance Rate Jump Tactics Worked Example
text.summary: Worked example: B2B SaaS onboarding niche, rate jump $100 -> $150.

section.title: Scenario
section.p: Solo dev pivoted to B2B SaaS onboarding rebuilds. 3 published case studies. Market median $140, p75 $175. Current rate $100. Existing client list = 4 clients on legacy rate.

section.title: Output
section.code: { "jump_id": "rj-2026q2-saas-onboarding", "niche": "B2B SaaS onboarding rebuild", "current_rate": 100, "target_rate": 150, "market_band": {"median": 140, "p75": 175, "sources": ["upwork-q1-2026", "codementor-rate-card", "peer-survey-2026"]}, "case_studies": [ {"customer": "Acme", "outcome": "+22% activation", "url": "https://example/acme"}, {"customer": "Beta", "outcome": "-40% time-to-value", "url": "https://example/beta"}, {"customer": "Gamma", "outcome": "+15 NPS", "url": "https://example/gamma"} ], "existing_client_notice": {"notice_days": 60, "wip_billing": "old-rate-until-complete"}, "rollout_plan": {"new_leads_at": "2026-06-01", "existing_clients_at": "2026-08-01"}, "owner": "@ruslan" }

list.item: Market band with 3 sources — rule market-band-citation.
WIP at old rate until complete — rule no-retroactive-billing.
